reasoning: Re-opened TCHSCR.MAC:701-739 (DOINTS + INTINT/INTSCR), file RADIX 16. Decoded the seed data myself. INTINT initials (A=1..Z=26, hex bytes): 0F,02,09=O(15)B(2)I(9)=OBI; 17,01,0E=W(23)A(1)N(14)=WAN; 08,01,0E=HAN; 07,0A,12=G(7)J(10)R(18)=GJR; 0D,0C,08=M(13)L(12)H(8)=MLH; 0A,05,04=JED; 0E,0C,01=NLA; 05,0A,04=EJD; 05,01,12=EAR; 12,0C,0D=R(18)L(12)M(13)=RLM. All ten match the transcription. INTSCR scores are BCD: each .WORD's hex digits are read literally as decimal BCD nibbles (4 bytes = 8 BCD digits per entry), so .WORD 0128,5353 -> bytes 01 28 53 53 -> '01285353' = 1,285,353. All ten decode exactly: 1,285,353 / 1,110,936 / 1,024,650 / 872,551 / 813,553 / 704,899 / 518,000 / 492,159 / 384,766 / 380,655. DOINTS copies these into HSCORS/INITLS on reset and CHKHIS falls back to DOINTS on EEROM corruption, so a fresh cabinet genuinely greets the player with OBI..RLM. Our side (render.ts:1037-1039) draws 'NO SCORES YET' on an empty board (localStorage-backed, no seeding). Real divergence; every transcribed name and value is correct.

VW8DIG (TCMES.MAC:788-793) unconditionally emits a VJNUMS comma glyph after the high digit-pair whenever a significant digit has appeared (LDA BT.SUP / IFLE / LDD #VJNUMS 'THEN SEPARATE NUMERALS WITH COMMAS'), and the same in VW6DIG. TCHSCR.MAC:224-238 draws each hi-score row via 'JSR VW8DIG ;VIEW 8 DIGITS WITH COMMAS' with BT.SUP=6 (suppress up to six leading zeros), so the ROM board is comma-grouped, zero-suppressed. Our side: render.ts:806/813 route the in-run HUD score through formatScore() (toLocaleString -> '128,535'), but the game-over line (render.ts:1009 `SCORE ${state.score}`) and the board rows (render.ts:1044 `String(e.score).padStart(6,' ')`) bypass it and print the bare integer. formatScore is already imported at render.ts:24, so the fix is a two-site reroute. Divergence real and specific; a 128535 board score shows '128,535' on the cabinet, '128535' here.

Re-opened VWGAS in WSGAS.MAC (file RADIX 16). The gauge colour is table-indexed by remaining energy: WSGAS.MAC:121-124 'LDB S.GAS / LSLB / LDX #TGCLR / LDU B(X)' (the outline colour), repeated at 149-152 for the numeral colour. TGCLR (WSGAS.MAC:285-294) is a 10-entry per-level ramp, decoded: S.GAS 0 = VGCOFF; 1-2 = VGCRED; 3-4 = VGCYLW; 5-9 = VGCGRN (each *100+VGCOPC). So the gauge ramps green->yellow->red->off as energy drains. Refuel brightening is real too: GSVALL (WSGAS.MAC:174-183) loads TGCLR then 'TST S.CAN / IFNE / ORB #0FF ;MAKE VERY BRIGHT'. This is the governing draw path each frame (no override; GS.HIT only substitutes VJFLS flash during a hit animation). Our render.ts:787 HUD_SHIELD_COLOR='#22e600' is a single green used unconditionally for the whole meter (render.ts:932-933,960-961) at every level. Real divergence. The finding's mechanism is correct but it did not decode the palette; supplied in corrections.
corrections: Decoded TGCLR palette (index = S.GAS): 0 -> VGCOFF (blanked), 1-2 -> VGCRED (critical), 3-4 -> VGCYLW (warning), 5-9 -> VGCGRN (healthy); colours carry VGCOPC. Refuel pulse: while S.CAN>0, the selected colour is OR'd with #0FF (GSVALL, WSGAS.MAC:179-181) to flash bright, decrementing each frame. A faithful ramp is thus three fixed hues (green/yellow/red) keyed on the shield count plus a brief bright-flash on pickup — no interpolation.

reasoning: Re-opened WSVROM.MAC:2449+. Verified the radix region: the file's .RADIX directives are :724->10., :1164->16., :1246->16 (after the WSVGAN include at :1235), so the DEATH STAR PICS section at :2449 is RADIX 16, and its AON/AOFF operands carry the decimal-suffix macro (per M-008, 'ABSOLUTE DECIMAL COORDS'), so decode as decimal deltas. The section is a full authentic Death Star vector picture set: BSHEM/BSHEM2 (VJ BHEM, green VGCGRN hemisphere, radius ~50, M.=32. detailed), BSCIR (green base circle), BSTRN (VGCWHT white equatorial-trench chord: AOFF 49,10 / AON -49,-9), BSDSH + BSNSD (VGCRED red superlaser dish, an offset ~17-radius circle around ~22,27) and BSFRM (VGCGRN green farmland surface hatching), with a BMIN miniature at M.=1. These are AVG billboard pictures scaled by the M. factor as the player closes, VG-RAM callable (VJ BHEM/VJ BMIN). Our side (models.ts:719 buildDeathStar) is a procedural 3D UV wireframe sphere (R=520, STACKS=8, SLICES=12) whose doc comment (models.ts:703-704) asserts 'no authentic vertex table exists for it'. That provenance claim is refuted by the primary source: authentic Death Star art does exist, and it is architecturally a scaling 2D picture, not a rotating lat/long sphere. Divergence real; size l (draw-path change + porting the point runs) is fair.
corrections: Minor terminology: the authentic scale factor is the AVG-picture multiplier `M.=32.` (detailed) / `M.=1` (BMIN miniature), not `.S` (`.S` is the 3D `.WP`-object scale). Value 32 and its role as the picture scale are correct; the finding's `.S=32` label is imprecise, not wrong in magnitude. Also note the ROM object is literally a 2D vector PICTURE (no 3D vertex table), so our comment's phrase 'no vertex table' is narrowly true yet misleading, since authentic Death Star art is present.

reasoning: Re-opened WSOBJ.MAC:576 (.WP WGA, WALL GUN TYPE A) — no .IF NE,0 guard (live object, in the same block as WPN/PORT which are confirmed shipped). Decoded the table at .S=8 (.P is decimal-suffix * scale): after the .P 0,0,0 anchor, 14 drawable points = a 4-corner wall base .P -32,0,24/32,0,24/32,0,-24/-32,0,-24 -> [-256,0,192]/[256,0,192]/[256,0,-192]/[-256,0,-192] (X +/-256, Z +/-192), a 6-pt gun body (.P -12,12,8.. -> up to [96,96,64]) and a 4-pt nozzle (.P -12,9,4.. -> [-96,72,32]). The draw routine .WGD WGA (WSOBJ.MAC:1780) is a real DRAWTO/BDRAWTO run over indices 1-14 with 'MOVD #VGCRED*100+VGCOPC' (drawn red); WGB shares the table via .WPZ2 WGB. Our side (models.ts:653 TRENCH_TURRET) is a provisional 10-vertex box+barrel (base +/-30, cap y=44, barrel y=72) whose comment (models.ts:644-651) claims 'No authentic vertex table is directly portable'. That claim is false: WGA is exactly a portable wall-gun table, and our provisional geometry, scale and vertex count bear no relation to it. Right table identified, model genuinely differs. Divergence real; a data re-port, size m.

Re-opened WSOBJ.MAC:603 (.WP WFF, WALL FORCE FIELD) — live object, no guard. Decoded at .S=8 (.PH is hex-suffix * scale): 6 points -> .PH -20,0,0/-20,40,0 = [-256,0,0]/[-256,512,0]; .PH 0,0,-20/0,40,-20 = [0,0,-256]/[0,512,-256]; .PH 0,0,20/0,40,20 = [0,0,256]/[0,512,256]. That is a tall 3-fin vertical barrier spanning the trench width (Z -256..+256) and rising y=0 to y=512 (0x40*8=512). Draw routine .WGD WFF (WSOBJ.MAC:1818, 'COLOR SET BY CALLER', DRAWTO 0,2,3,1,5,4,0) and its collided twin .WGD WFG (WSOBJ.MAC:1830, .WPZ2 WFG). The decisive evidence the auditor cited holds verbatim: .WGD WFG carries 'MOVD #VJFLS ;CATWALK COLOR WHEN COLLIDED' — the ROM's own comment ties WFF/WFG to the trench catwalk (it flashes VJFLS on collision). Our side (models.ts:688 TRENCH_CATWALK) is a provisional flat 8-vertex horizontal girder (X +/-256, Y +/-12, Z 0..-24) marked PROVISIONAL. The authentic WFF (tall vertical 3-fin) differs from our flat girder in both geometry and orientation. Right table identified via the ROM's collided-catwalk comment; model genuinely differs. Divergence real, size m.
